ogTitle: Kentucky South vs Keri Keri: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kentucky South and Keri Keri.
heading: Kentucky South vs Keri Keri — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kentucky South, New South Wales and Keri Keri, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Kentucky South has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Keri Keri (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kentucky South or Keri Keri safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kentucky South has a flood risk score of 43/100, while Keri Keri scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kentucky South scores 50/100 for bushfire risk. Keri Keri scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kentucky South or Keri Keri?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Kentucky South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
